rows.test: Code review of reserve-run-dir.sh: candidate number is only a hint; actual claim is via mkdir, retried on failure regardless of what ls showed

rows.requirement: Atomic reservation via mkdir + EEXIST retry
rows.test: reserve-run-dir.sh implementation loop: mkdir "$dir" 2>/dev/null && { echo "$dir"; exit 0; } || candidate incremented and retried

rows.requirement: Usable by both implementation and verification dispatch paths
rows.test: Script takes agent-suffix as first arg (implementation|verification|planner|compaction|commit|documentation|push all work identically)
test-reserve-run-dir.sh spawns 20 backgrounded calls against an isolated temp dir, asserts 20 unique existing directories
test-reserve-run-dir.sh creates a fresh temp dir per run (mktemp -d) and cleans up on exit; safe to re-run

rows.requirement: Skill/documentation updated to reference the helper
rows.test: Pointer note added to cdr-runtime agents/cdr/implementation.md and verification.md (out-of-band, separate repo) referencing .cdr/tools/reserve-run-dir.sh
Script only creates new directories under caller-supplied base dir; test suite uses an isolated temp dir, never the real .cdr/runs/
